summary: art-2026-03-21-014 shows real task-specific signal, but its strongest tension is between precise-looking rankings and a production comparison that was not cleanly standardized.
review_confidence: high
contradictions:
  - contradiction_text: Gemini CLI had the largest stated context window and later completed the backend refactor on the first try after loading broad codebase context, yet it performed worst in the blank-repo app-generation task.
    related_claim_texts:
      - Gemini CLI’s very large context window did not translate into top performance in the blank-repo task, even though it later completed the backend refactor on the first try after loading broad codebase context.
    related_fact_refs:
      - fact-003
      - fact-010
      - fact-013
    related_evidence_ids:
      - evidence-03
      - evidence-10
      - evidence-13
    disposition: bounded
    disclosure: Large context capacity may help repository-oriented work, but this material does not support treating context-window size as a general predictor of coding-agent performance across task types.
    confidence: high
  - contradiction_text: The benchmark presents precise final cross-tool averages even though the production-code comparison used different tasks for different tools and was scored mainly by overall impression rather than identical execution.
    related_claim_texts:
      - The benchmark’s final cross-tool rankings should be treated cautiously because the production-code comparison used different tasks for different tools and was scored mainly by overall impression rather than identical execution.
    related_fact_refs:
